rationale.completeness: Fully addresses all requirements: accepts multiple tickers, calculates relative performance (total/annualized return, best/worst days), computes risk metrics (volatility, Sharpe, max drawdown, Sortino), and builds correlation matrix with Pearson coefficients. SKILL.md provides comprehensive workflow documentation.
rationale.correctness: Uses correct financial formulas (geometric mean for annualized return, sample std dev, Pearson correlation). Minor type inconsistency: endDate is assigned tradingDays (number) but typed as string in PerformanceMetrics interface.
rationale.style: Follows TypeScript conventions with proper types, consistent camelCase naming, good JSDoc comments, and functional programming patterns. Matches project style.
rationale.quality: Well-organized with clear separation of concerns (performance, risk, correlation modules), good modularity, comprehensive tests covering edge cases, and proper error handling for empty arrays and insufficient data.
